torch_pattern:
q = ttnn.linear(x, self.wq, ...)
k = ttnn.linear(x, self.wk, ...)
v = ttnn.linear(x, self.wv, ...)
# (torch-level: q = x @ Wq; k = x @ Wk; v = x @ Wv  — N sibling projections of the same input)

config_template.call: xqkv_fused = ttnn.linear(x, self.wqkv, memory_config={MEM_CONFIG}, dtype={DTYPE}, program_config={PROGRAM_CONFIG}, compute_kernel_config={COMPUTE_KERNEL_CONFIG})  # followed by ttnn.experimental.nlp_create_qkv_heads(xqkv_fused, num_heads={NUM_HEADS}, num_kv_heads={NUM_KV_HEADS}) to split
config_template.notes: Applies to any N sibling linears sharing one input: q/k/v -> wqkv (split via nlp_create_qkv_heads), and is the same merge family as key/value -> fused_kv (whisper parameters.key_value). w1/w3 (gate/up) pairs in MLPs are kept as two linears in most call sites but share identical program/memory configs and are merge candidates.

weight_transform: wqkv = torch.cat([Wq.T, Wk.T, Wv.T], dim=-1) — transpose each torch [out, in] weight to [in, out], then concatenate along the output (last) dim; biases concatenated likewise. Output must be split per-head afterwards (nlp_create_qkv_heads / nlp_create_qkv_heads_decode), and KV-group-interleaved layouts require the matching rearranged wqkv.

placement_observations.size_descriptor: fused QKV decode output, M=32 rows
fused QKV prefill output, seq_len chunked at MAX_QKV_MM_SEQ_LEN
placement_observations.source: prefill: `if seq_len > self.MAX_QKV_MM_SEQ_LEN: x_11SH = ttnn.reshape(...)`; xqkv_fused = ttnn.linear(x_11SH, self.wqkv, memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG, program_config=XQKV_PREFILL_PROGCFG(seq_len))

placement_observations.size_descriptor: whisper fused key_value projection input, moved to L1 before merge matmul
placement_observations.source: key_value_states = ttnn.to_memory_config(key_value_states, ttnn.L1_MEMORY_CONFIG); fused_kv = ttnn.linear(key_value_states, parameters.key_value.weight, bias=..., core_grid=full_grid, memory_config=ttnn.L1_MEMORY_CONFIG)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: gate/up sibling pair in decode, DRAM-sharded weights, identical shard layout for both outputs (enables sharded ttnn.mul)
placement_observations.source: gate_sharded = ttnn.linear(x_sharded, gate_proj_dram_sharded, ...); up_sharded = ttnn.linear(x_sharded, up_proj_dram_sharded, ...) with identical program/memory configs — 'gate/up sharding == down in0 sharding (same K). Mul preserves layout.'
